    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Erd≈ës Problem 995
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/995](https://www.erdosproblems.com/995)
    23‚Üí
    24‚Üí[Er49d] Erd≈ës, P. (1949) ‚Äî constructed a lacunary sequence and $f \in L^2([0,1])$ such that, for
    25‚Üíevery $\varepsilon > 0$, for almost all $\alpha$,
    26‚Üí$\limsup_{N \to \infty} \frac{1}{N(\log \log N)^{1/2 - \varepsilon}} \sum_{k \leq N}
    27‚Üíf(\{\alpha n_k\}) = \infty$.
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üí[Er64b] Erd≈ës, P. (1964) ‚Äî thought that his lower bound was closer to the truth.
    30‚Üí-/
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üíopen MeasureTheory Filter Finset Set
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üínamespace Erdos995
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üí/-- A sequence of positive integers is lacunary if it is strictly increasing
    37‚Üíand there exists $q > 1$ such that $n_{k+1} \geq q \cdot n_k$ for all $k$. -/
    38‚Üídef IsLacunary (n : ‚Ñï ‚Üí ‚Ñï) : Prop :=
    39‚Üí  StrictMono n ‚àß ‚àÉ q : ‚Ñù, q > 1 ‚àß ‚àÄ k : ‚Ñï, (n k : ‚Ñù) * q ‚â§ n (k + 1)
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí/--
    42‚ÜíErd≈ës Problem 995 [Er64b]:
    43‚Üí
    44‚ÜíIs it true that for every lacunary sequence $(n_k)$ of positive integers and every
    45‚Üí$f \in L^2([0,1])$, for almost all $\alpha \in [0,1]$,
    46‚Üí$$\sum_{k < N} f(\{\alpha \cdot n_k\}) = o(N \cdot \sqrt{\log \log N})?$$
    47‚Üí
    48‚ÜíFormulated as: for every $\varepsilon > 0$, for almost all $\alpha$, eventually (for large $N$),
    49‚Üí$\left|\sum_{k < N} f(\{\alpha \cdot n_k\})\right| \leq \varepsilon \cdot N \cdot
    50‚Üí\sqrt{\log \log N}$.
    51‚Üí-/
    52‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 11 42]
    53‚Üítheorem erdos_995 : answer(sorry) ‚Üî
    54‚Üí    ‚àÄ (n : ‚Ñï ‚Üí ‚Ñï), IsLacunary n ‚Üí
    55‚Üí    ‚àÄ (f : ‚Ñù ‚Üí ‚Ñù), Measurable f ‚Üí
    56‚Üí    Integrable (fun x => f x ^ 2) (volume.restrict (Icc (0 : ‚Ñù) 1)) ‚Üí

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erd≈ës Problem 995

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/995](https://www.erdosproblems.com/995)

[Er49d] Erd≈ës, P. (1949) ‚Äî constructed a lacunary sequence and $f \in L^2([0,1])$ such that, for
every $\varepsilon > 0$, for almost all $\alpha$,
$\limsup_{N \to \infty} \frac{1}{N(\log \log N)^{1/2 - \varepsilon}} \sum_{k \leq N}
f(\{\alpha n_k\}) = \infty$.

[Er64b] Erd≈ës, P. (1964) ‚Äî thought that his lower bound was closer to the truth.
-/

open MeasureTheory Filter Finset Set

namespace Erdos995

/-- A sequence of positive integers is lacunary if it is strictly increasing
and there exists $q > 1$ such that $n_{k+1} \geq q \cdot n_k$ for all $k$. -/
def IsLacunary (n : ‚Ñï ‚Üí ‚Ñï) : Prop :=
  StrictMono n ‚àß ‚àÉ q : ‚Ñù, q > 1 ‚àß ‚àÄ k : ‚Ñï, (n k : ‚Ñù) * q ‚â§ n (k + 1)

/--
Erd≈ës Problem 995 [Er64b]:

Is it true that for every lacunary sequence $(n_k)$ of positive integers and every
$f \in L^2([0,1])$, for almost all $\alpha \in [0,1]$,
$$\sum_{k < N} f(\{\alpha \cdot n_k\}) = o(N \cdot \sqrt{\log \log N})?$$

Formulated as: for every $\varepsilon > 0$, for almost all $\alpha$, eventually (for large $N$),
$\left|\sum_{k < N} f(\{\alpha \cdot n_k\})\right| \leq \varepsilon \cdot N \cdot
\sqrt{\log \log N}$.
-/
@[category research open, AMS 11 42]
theorem erdos_995 : answer(sorry) ‚Üî
    ‚àÄ (n : ‚Ñï ‚Üí ‚Ñï), IsLacunary n ‚Üí
    ‚àÄ (f : ‚Ñù ‚Üí ‚Ñù), Measurable f ‚Üí
    Integrable (fun x => f x ^ 2) (volume.restrict (Icc (0 : ‚Ñù) 1)) ‚Üí
    ‚àÄ Œµ : ‚Ñù, Œµ > 0 ‚Üí
    ‚àÄ·µê Œ± ‚àÇ(volume.restrict (Icc (0 : ‚Ñù) 1)),
      ‚àÉ N‚ÇÄ : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ N : ‚Ñï, N ‚â• N‚ÇÄ ‚Üí
        |‚àë k ‚àà Finset.range N, f (Int.fract (Œ± * (n k : ‚Ñù)))| ‚â§
          Œµ * (N : ‚Ñù) * Real.sqrt (Real.log (Real.log (N : ‚Ñù))) := by
  sorry

end Erdos995

     2‚Üí
     3‚Üí## 1. Copyright Header
     4‚Üí- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
     5‚Üí- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.
     6‚Üí

     9‚Üí- [x] No other imports are present.
    10‚Üí
    11‚Üí## 3. Module Docstring
    12‚Üí- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
    13‚Üí- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erd≈ës Problem 995`.
    14‚Üí- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/995`.
    15‚Üí- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem.
    16‚Üí- [x] Bibliography entries present: `[Er49d]` and `[Er64b]`.
    17‚Üí
    18‚Üí## 4. Namespace
    19‚Üí- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos995 ... end Erdos995`.
    20‚Üí- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos995`).
    21‚Üí- [x] `open` statements (`open MeasureTheory Filter Finset Set`) appear **before** the namespace block.
    22‚Üí
    23‚Üí## 5. Custom Definitions
    24‚Üí- [x] `IsLacunary` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
    25‚Üí- [x] No `noncomputable` needed (definition is a `Prop`).
    26‚Üí- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
    27‚Üí- [x] `IsLacunary` uses `UpperCamelCase` (it is a `Prop`/type).
    28‚Üí- [x] No local notation used (none needed).
    29‚Üí- [x] No `@[category test]` sanity check (optional ‚Äî acceptable).
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí## 6. Theorem Statements
    32‚Üí- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
    33‚Üí- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_995` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
    34‚Üí- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib conventions: `snake_case` for terms of Props.
    35‚Üí- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 11 42]`.
    36‚Üí- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
    37‚Üí- [x] Docstring references source paper: `[Er64b]`.
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí### `answer()` usage
    40‚ÜíThe problem asks "Is it true that...?" ‚Äî a yes/no question (**Case 1**).
    41‚Üí- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ‚Üî P` (correct for Case 1).
    42‚Üí- [x] The RHS formalizes the question as originally asked (affirmative answer = `True`).
    43‚Üí- [x] Quantifiers (`‚àÄ n`, `‚àÄ f`, `‚àÄ Œµ`, `‚àÄ·µê Œ±`) go **after** `answer(sorry)` on the RHS, not as theorem parameters.
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí## 7. Category Attribute
    46‚Üí- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
    47‚Üí- [x] Value is `research open` ‚Äî valid category.
    48‚Üí- [x] `research open` theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body.
    49‚Üí
    50‚Üí## 8. AMS Attribute
    51‚Üí- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute (not multiple separate tags).
    52‚Üí- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (two: `11`, `42`).
    53‚Üí- [x] Numbers are in ascending numeric order: `11 42`.
    54‚Üí- [x] No duplicate numbers.
    55‚Üí- [x] Both `11` (Number theory) and `42` (Harmonic analysis) are valid AMS MSC2020 codes.
    56‚Üí
    57‚Üí## 9. Proof Bodies
    58‚Üí- [x] Open conjecture uses `by\n  sorry` (acceptable multi-line format).
    59‚Üí- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
    60‚Üí- [x] No informal proof sketches for the open research problem.
    61‚Üí- [x] No `native_decide`.
    62‚Üí
    63‚Üí## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems
    64‚Üí- N/A ‚Äî no variants present.
    65‚Üí
    66‚Üí## 11. Code Quality
    67‚Üí- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
    68‚Üí- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable (`‚àÄ`, `‚àÉ`, `Œµ`, `‚àà`, `‚â•`, `‚â§`, `‚Ñï`, `‚Ñù`).
    69‚Üí- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
    70‚Üí- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
    71‚Üí- [x] Lines are reasonably short (<100 characters).
    72‚Üí- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.
    73‚Üí
    74‚Üí## 12. Build Verification
    75‚Üí- [ ] `lake build` not run ‚Äî cannot verify in this review.
    76‚Üí- [ ] Custom linter pass not verified.
    77‚Üí
    78‚Üí## Mathematical Notes
    79‚Üí
    80‚Üí- **Lacunary sequence**: The `IsLacunary` definition captures `StrictMono n` with existence of `q > 1` such that `n(k) ¬∑ q ‚â§ n(k+1)`. This correctly formalizes the standard notion. Note that `n 0 = 0` is technically possible (not excluded), but this is harmless since the fractional part `Int.fract(Œ± ¬∑ 0) = 0` just contributes `f(0)` to the sum, which doesn't affect the asymptotic.
    81‚Üí- **L¬≤ condition**: Formalized as `Measurable f` plus `Integrable (fun x => f x ^ 2) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1))`. This is a reasonable formalization of `f ‚àà L¬≤([0,1])`.
    82‚Üí- **Little-o formulation**: The statement "‚àÄ Œµ > 0, ‚àÄ·µê Œ±, ‚àÉ N‚ÇÄ, ‚àÄ N ‚â• N‚ÇÄ, |sum| ‚â§ Œµ ¬∑ N ¬∑ ‚àö(log log N)" correctly encodes the little-o condition `o(N ¬∑ ‚àö(log log N))`.
    83‚Üí
    84‚Üí## Verdict
    85‚Üí
    86‚Üí**PASS** ‚Äî all checkable items conform. Clean, well-structured file.
    87‚Üí

# Review: deepmind/995.lean

## 1. Copyright Header
- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement
- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring
- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erd≈ës Problem 995`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/995`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem.
- [x] Bibliography entries present: `[Er49d]` and `[Er64b]`.

## 4. Namespace
- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos995 ... end Erdos995`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos995`).
- [x] `open` statements (`open MeasureTheory Filter Finset Set`) appear **before** the namespace block.

## 5. Custom Definitions
- [x] `IsLacunary` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] No `noncomputable` needed (definition is a `Prop`).
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] `IsLacunary` uses `UpperCamelCase` (it is a `Prop`/type).
- [x] No local notation used (none needed).
- [x] No `@[category test]` sanity check (optional ‚Äî acceptable).

## 6. Theorem Statements
- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_995` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib conventions: `snake_case` for terms of Props.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 11 42]`.
- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] Docstring references source paper: `[Er64b]`.

### `answer()` usage
The problem asks "Is it true that...?" ‚Äî a yes/no question (**Case 1**).
- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ‚Üî P` (correct for Case 1).
- [x] The RHS formalizes the question as originally asked (affirmative answer = `True`).
- [x] Quantifiers (`‚àÄ n`, `‚àÄ f`, `‚àÄ Œµ`, `‚àÄ·µê Œ±`) go **after** `answer(sorry)` on the RHS, not as theorem parameters.

## 7. Category Attribute
- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
- [x] Value is `research open` ‚Äî valid category.
- [x] `research open` theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body.

## 8. AMS Attribute
- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute (not multiple separate tags).
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (two: `11`, `42`).
- [x] Numbers are in ascending numeric order: `11 42`.
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] Both `11` (Number theory) and `42` (Harmonic analysis) are valid AMS MSC2020 codes.

## 9. Proof Bodies
- [x] Open conjecture uses `by\n  sorry` (acceptable multi-line format).
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for the open research problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems
- N/A ‚Äî no variants present.

## 11. Code Quality
- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable (`‚àÄ`, `‚àÉ`, `Œµ`, `‚àà`, `‚â•`, `‚â§`, `‚Ñï`, `‚Ñù`).
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (<100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification
- [ ] `lake build` not run ‚Äî cannot verify in this review.
- [ ] Custom linter pass not verified.

## Mathematical Notes

- **Lacunary sequence**: The `IsLacunary` definition captures `StrictMono n` with existence of `q > 1` such that `n(k) ¬∑ q ‚â§ n(k+1)`. This correctly formalizes the standard notion. Note that `n 0 = 0` is technically possible (not excluded), but this is harmless since the fractional part `Int.fract(Œ± ¬∑ 0) = 0` just contributes `f(0)` to the sum, which doesn't affect the asymptotic.
- **L¬≤ condition**: Formalized as `Measurable f` plus `Integrable (fun x => f x ^ 2) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1))`. This is a reasonable formalization of `f ‚àà L¬≤([0,1])`.
- **Little-o formulation**: The statement "‚àÄ Œµ > 0, ‚àÄ·µê Œ±, ‚àÉ N‚ÇÄ, ‚àÄ N ‚â• N‚ÇÄ, |sum| ‚â§ Œµ ¬∑ N ¬∑ ‚àö(log log N)" correctly encodes the little-o condition `o(N ¬∑ ‚àö(log log N))`.

## Verdict

**PASS** ‚Äî all checkable items conform. Clean, well-structured file.

content.text: The review verdict is **PASS**. No changes needed.
